title: Lego Man in the White Void
synopsis: A single LEGO minifigure alone in an infinite white space, doing whatever comes to mind — dancing, playing with props, trying tricks, failing, trying again. No story, no destination. Just a minifigure finding things to do in a featureless void, with the user tossing ideas his way.
visual_style: Studio product-shot aesthetic. The frame is pure white — no background, no horizon, no floor seam, no shadows except a very subtle soft contact shadow directly under the minifigure. Soft even lighting from all directions like a photography tent. The minifigure is the only fixed element on screen; props come and go as the story requires. Motion has a slight stop-motion feel — every click, snap, and pop-off reads clearly. No on-screen text, no camera flourish. Cuts and zooms only when they genuinely serve a gag.
tone_guidelines: David-Attenborough-style nature-documentary voice-over, applied earnestly to a single LEGO minifigure. Warm, observational, treats small physical comedy with the same quiet gravitas you'd give a pride of lions on the savannah. Dry humor works because delivery stays straight — never winking, never nudging the audience. Short bursts only, leaving silence for the action to breathe. Whispered asides when something delicate happens; full-voice when the minifigure lands a bit. Never breaks the fourth wall, never acknowledges it's plastic or a simulation.

locations.name: The White Void
locations.description: An infinite, featureless white space. No floor line, no horizon, no walls. Soft shadowless studio lighting from all directions. Objects and props exist only when the story summons them; otherwise the space is empty except for the minifigure. Props enter in one of two ways: (1) they simply appear in frame, or (2) the minifigure walks a few steps out of frame and returns carrying the new prop.

characters.name: Lego Man
characters.description: The protagonist and only character in this world. The most default minifigure imaginable: plain yellow head with the classic two-dot eyes and curved smile, plain red torso with no print, plain blue legs, claw hands, short black classic-hair piece. He never speaks — only acts, reacts, and emotes through posture, body language, head tilts, and physical comedy.

world_constraints: This is a LEGO world. The minifigure is made of plastic.

LEGO anatomy is absolute: claw hands that grip but can't point or do fine motor tasks, legs that do not bend at the knee, the head rotates 360° but has one permanently printed expression, and parts (hair, hats, limbs, head) can pop off and click back on.
The minifigure never speaks. No dialogue, no voiceover, no on-screen text. All humor and emotion comes from body language and physical comedy.
There is only one character. No other minifigures ever appear, by any mechanism. Non-human props and creatures are fine (a plastic dog, a plastic butterfly, a cardboard box).
The background is always pure white — featureless, infinite, shadowless. The story never relocates to another place; all variety comes from props and actions, not from environment changes.

Props enter the scene in one of two ways: (1) they simply appear in frame, or (2) the minifigure walks a few steps out of frame and returns carrying the new prop. Both are valid; mix them for variety.
Props that are still in play should be remembered and can be revisited turns later. Props can also be removed (disassembled into bricks, thrown off-screen, packed away).
narrative_premise: A minifigure alone in a white void does whatever comes to mind — dance, play, try tricks, fail, try again. Props and bits come and go. The fun comes from callbacks: the soccer ball from early on reappears, the hat changes, gags escalate. Earnest, warm, physical-comedy humor. Never cynical.
long_term_narrative: Bits escalate in absurdity across the session, and props introduced earlier are remembered and revisited — the longer the session goes, the more the minifigure's world of accumulated toys and running gags has to build on.
short_term_narrative: The minifigure wakes up in the empty white space and starts figuring out what to do with himself.
